diagrams.description: Public Relations is the strategic management of communication and relationships between an organization and its publics. This flowchart outlines the discipline’s logic: the ultimate goal is to build trust, credibility, and mutually beneficial relationships. It integrates core areas: earned media and influencer relations (media outreach, journalist partnerships, community engagement), strategic corporate communication (crisis management, issues and change communication, internal relations, public affairs), and content-driven digital strategy (social media, storytelling, events). These are grounded in major theoretical frameworks—Excellence theory, situational theory of publics, framing, agenda‑building, and dialogic communication—and unfold through the strategic process cycle (Research → Action → Communication → Evaluation, RACE). The resulting outcomes include reputation enhancement, stakeholder loyalty, issue resolution, and long‑term organizational legitimacy.
